scope: migration path from current GameState toward deterministic living-world kernel
questions: Can the long-term AI-driven open-world direction be staged without changing current Phase 1 gameplay?
What is the safest first implementation spike after RFC approval?
Which architecture boundaries are required to keep AI proposals testable and reversible?
acceptance: Technical review is submitted before Draft PR creation.
Review keeps AI behind schema validation and authoritative simulation.
Review recommends no implementation work until the RFC is accepted or revised.
result: Codex preliminary feasibility review: viable as a long-term direction only if simulation-first. Recommended first spike is an AI Council Chamber using fixed-seed tests, deterministic fallback agents, structured WorldAction proposals, ActionValidator, authoritative execution, and append-only event logs. Do not start full 3D open-world work or model-dependent gameplay from this RFC.
